app.name: Indusia Visual Editor
app.tagline: PCB inspection, from BOM to production in hours

auth.loginHint: Use the credentials provided by your team admin.
auth.signupHint: The first account automatically becomes the organization admin.
auth.signupSubtitle: First-time setup for your factory operator.
auth.signupBrandHook: Start your PCB inspection in 6 minutes.
auth.signupBrandSub: Sign up free for your MI factory team. 14-day trial, unlimited projects, no credit card required.
auth.tncAgree: I agree to Indusia's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
auth.brandHook: From BOM to production inspection in hours.
auth.brandSub: Operator uploads BOM + golden sample, Gemma generates the inspection plan, you approve at 2 HITL gates. No YAML, no CLI.
auth.step2Sub: Gemma generates, you approve at Gate 1
auth.signupHook: Train your first PCB inspector this afternoon.
auth.bullet1: Upload BOM + golden sample · no YAML, no CLI
auth.bullet2: Two HITL gates before any model touches the line
auth.bullet3: Built for MI operators, not data scientists
auth.signupBullet1: Sign up under your factory's organization slug
auth.signupBullet2: First account becomes admin by default
auth.signupBullet3: Switch to Bahasa Indonesia anytime from the top bar

dashboard.subhead: Pick up where you left off or start a fresh PCB.
dashboard.empty: No projects yet — start your first PCB to begin.
dashboard.qs2: Label defect regions with AI assist · ~30 min
dashboard.qs3: Approve Gate 1, train, review metrics · ~60 min
dashboard.qs4: Promote model to edge via Gate 2
dashboard.advisorBlurb: Ask Gemma anything about your current run — open the chat anywhere.
dashboard.allProjectsSub: Pick a project to continue the inspection workflow.
dashboard.emptyDesc: Start by uploading a BOM (Excel/CSV) and golden sample photos of both PCB sides. Gemma generates the inspection plan automatically.

wizard.title: Set up your PCB inspection
wizard.s1Blurb: Name the project. The slug is auto-generated from the name.
wizard.s2Blurb: Drop a SAP ZLMM_BOM_REPORT export (.xls) or Excel/CSV. The parser flattens the hierarchy, expands multi-designator rows, and auto-detects MI-likely components.
wizard.s3Blurb: Upload one good top-side and bottom-side photo. The AI uses these as a visual reference.
wizard.s4Blurb: Upload a PCB layout drawing (JPG/PNG). Required — the AI needs it as a spatial prior for pre-label.
wizard.s5Blurb: Check that everything is correct. Edit any step before creating the project.
wizard.slugHint: Lowercase, numbers, dashes only. Used in URLs and storage paths.
wizard.uploadBom: Drop BOM file or click to choose
wizard.goldenBottomOptional: Optional — skip if the bottom side has no inspectable components.
wizard.goldenBottomMissingDetail: For full MI inspection, both PCB sides should be photographed. Skip → AI can only inspect the top side.
wizard.photoTip1Body: Use softbox or ambient light — avoid harsh shadows.
wizard.photoTip2Body: Camera dead-on, not tilted. Use a jig if possible.
wizard.photoTip3Body: Make sure the 3 corner marks aren't cropped out.
wizard.uploadDrawing: Drop drawing or click to choose
wizard.drawingNote: JPG / PNG only · Gerber and ODB++ are out of scope in v1
wizard.drawingRequired: Drawing is required before labeling can start.
wizard.reviewNote: Continue to labeling — you can re-upload assets anytime from the canvas.
wizard.errMissingProject: Create the project first before uploading files.
wizard.bomUploadedAwaitingParse: BOM uploaded — parsing on the server…
wizard.nameHelp: Use a name your QA team will recognize.
wizard.chk3: Golden samples — top + bottom side photo
wizard.chk5: Review — confirm all data is correct
wizard.bomDropParsed: Parsed successfully. Click to re-upload or drag a new file.
wizard.miNoteBody: {n} components flagged MI-likely (through-hole + wave solder) by the heuristic classifier. Override per-component in the labeling canvas.
wizard.goldenBothDetail: Top + bottom golden samples ready. Click Continue to proceed to the PCB drawing.
wizard.drawingDropHint: Max 50 MB · Component placement view recommended
wizard.drawingHelpsTitle: How the drawing helps the AI:
wizard.drawingSavedBody: The AI will use this drawing as a spatial prior for more precise component pre-label positions.
wizard.next1Body: BOM parsed, golden samples saved to storage.
wizard.next2Body: Operator annotates regions on the golden sample (LSF).
wizard.next3Body: Approve labels → the AI trains the model.
wizard.next4Body: Review metrics → push to the production edge.

gate2.bannerReady: Run {id} ready · final confirmation before push to edge
gate2.bannerReadyBlurb: All thresholds passed — the model is ready to push to the inspection edges.
gate2.title: Confirm model deployment
gate2.subhead: Push the trained model to all registered edges. This is the last manual step.

banner.passed: Eval passed all thresholds — safe to promote.
banner.passedBlurb: mAP, F1 macro, and per-component F1 are all green.
banner.blocked: Eval did not pass — cannot promote yet.
banner.blockedBlurb: Return to evaluation, fix failing components, and re-run training.

gate2.step1: Tag the model with the registry version and SHA256.
gate2.step2: Push the binary to the on-prem registry via the ais CLI.
gate2.step3: Fire webhooks to every online edge — pinned edges keep their override.
gate2.noEdges: No edges registered yet. Add one before promoting.
gate2.offlineWarn: {n} edge(s) offline and will receive the model on next reconnect.
gate2.rollbackTarget: Previous production version stays available — use `ais model pin <pcb> <version>` from Edges to revert.
gate2.confirmRow: I have reviewed the metrics and confirm this model is safe to deploy.
gate2.confirmRowBlurb: Required before the Promote button is enabled.
gate2.promoteSuccessBody: Version {version} is live on all online edges.

modal.title: Promote model to production?
modal.body: This will push the binary to the registry and fire webhooks to every online edge. The action cannot be undone — use rollback from Edges if needed.

models.title: Models registry
models.subhead: All trained models across projects, with their promotion + pin state.

edges.title: Edge devices
edges.subhead: All cameras and PLCs receiving model pushes. Pin a version to override auto-pull.
edges.unpinSuccess: Edge unpinned — will auto-pull on next webhook.

datasets.title: Datasets
datasets.subhead: Training splits, holdouts, and edge production runs.

team.title: Team
team.subhead: Members of your organization. Admins can invite new users.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.subhead: Personal settings — stored on this device only.
preferences.languageBlurb: Affects UI copy only. Engineering jargon (mAP, F1) stays English.
preferences.engineerBlurb: Reveal hyperparameters, tech details, and log terminals on workflow screens.
preferences.engineerMovedHint: Engineer mode now lives in the top bar — toggle the </> chip to enable.
preferences.storedLocal: These preferences are stored in localStorage and do not sync across devices.

chat.title: Ask Gemma
chat.placeholder: Ask about the current run, dataset, or any decision…
chat.shortcut: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for new line
chat.openProjectFirst: Open a project first — the advisor is project-scoped.
chat.empty: Ask the advisor about your training run, BOM, or eval thresholds.

gate1.title: Training preparation
gate1.subhead: Review dataset readiness, training mode, and considerations before approving the run.
gate1.hitlBlurb: Review dataset readiness, then approve to start. You can cancel any time.
gate1.modeScratchBlurb: Train a fresh model using only this project's labels.
gate1.modeContinueTooltip: No previous model yet. Start from scratch first.
gate1.consid1: MI components (J5, U7, C4) need at least 15 regions each before quality is stable.
gate1.consid2: Per-component F1 must reach 0.70 before promote — review at-risk rows below.
gate1.consid3: Training is non-destructive — you can re-train as often as needed.
gate1.readinessLine: You have prepared {total} annotations across {n} components.
gate1.considAtRisk: {d} has only {n} examples. Add more annotations or skip it from inspection.
gate1.considModerate: Borderline coverage: {list}. Adding 10–15 more each improves reliability.
gate1.considCoverage: Coverage is at {pct}% across labeled components.
gate1.considAllGood: All labeled components have sufficient coverage. Good to train.
gate1.modeSub: Choose how to train the model.
gate1.modeScratchSub: Train a new model from YOLOv8n base weights. Best for first-time training.
gate1.readyBlurb: Training is non-destructive and can be cancelled any time. Model deployment happens later at Gate 2.

training.title: Training in progress
training.noComponents: Waiting for the first epoch event…
training.thing1: Per-component F1 below 0.70 will surface in evaluation.
training.thing2: Metrics refresh on every epoch event from the training service.
training.thing3: Stop-early and hyperparameter details are available in Engineer mode.

setupEval.title: Setup evaluation
setupEval.subhead: Pick a test set and start. Eval is fully manual — results land on the eval review screen when finished.
setupEval.hitlTitle: Manual step — operator picks the test set
setupEval.hitlBlurb: Eval is never auto-run. After you click Start, the model is locked at this version while it scores every image in the chosen set.
setupEval.testSetSub: Hold-out images the model has never seen during training.
setupEval.thing1: Promote stays locked until every gate passes.
setupEval.thing2: You can rerun eval as many times as needed.

holdout.title: Holdout (training split)
holdout.blurb: Fixed 20% held back from the training run. Best for apples-to-apples comparison.

production_run.title: Latest production run
production_run.blurb: Last 24h of edge predictions, hand-labeled by the operator.

upload.title: Upload ZIP
upload.blurb: Bring your own test set — coming soon.

eval.title: Eval results
eval.failingBlurb: One or more components fell below threshold. Click a failing tile to open correction mode.
eval.correctedBlurb: Run a fresh training pass with the updated labels.
eval.passedBlurb: All thresholds met. Continue to Gate 2 to deploy the model.
eval.failingDetail: {d} F1 {f1} is below the {threshold} threshold. Correct the wrong samples → retrain (fine-tune) → re-eval.
eval.runSub: Scored on a hold-out set of {n} predictions.

labeling.title: Labeling canvas
labeling.selectHint: Click a region on the canvas to see its details here.
labeling.correctionMessage: {count} samples flagged for correction. Edit each region then save.
labeling.emptyHint: Upload a BOM and golden samples before labeling can start.
labeling.workflowTip: Draw a rectangle, pick the designator, mark defect criteria, then save. Use Tab to cycle regions.
labeling.correctionBanner: Correction mode · {count} flagged samples from Run #{run} filtered to the thumbnail rail
labeling.regionEmptyHint: Select a region to inspect properties + actions.
